tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The visible tests are predominantly text/structural checks: `ast.parse()` for syntax, `yaml.safe_load()` for YAML validity, string searches for wildcard imports, ruff lint/format checks, trailing whitespace checks, newline checks. None of the visible tests actually import and call `format_reward()`, `acc_reward()`, or `geometry3k_reward_fn()` to verify behavioral correctness. test_not_tautological.reason: The pass_to_pass tests (syntax, lint, format, YAML validity) would fail if the agent creates files with syntax errors, lint violations, or invalid YAML. A stub empty file wouldn't pass ruff format/lint checks if it has wrong imports. The f2p tests (file existence, content checks) require actual file creation with proper content.

An agent could read /solution/solve.sh (if mounted) or inspect the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to reverse-engineer the expected output.
